title: aporrhiza talbotii
intro: Aporrhiza talbotii, also known as Talbot's coral bush, is a shrub native to Australia. It is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapindaceae, and its name is derived from its unique, coral-like red fruits. Aporrhiza talbotii has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic acids with potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and offer antioxidant properties, which can be accessed through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
